---
title: "LBSR Phases 2–3: Evidence ingestion and diagnostics"
output: html_document
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{LBSR Phases 2–3: Evidence ingestion and diagnostics}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This vignette documents the Phase 2–3 workflow. It intentionally stops before
synthetic respondent generation.

```{r, eval=FALSE}
library(lbsr)
project <- create_lbsr_project("rec_case", "REC legacy reconstruction", "Research team")
project <- register_sources(project, "REC_reconstruction_master_coding_book.xlsx",
                            "coding_book", "derived_primary_input")
bundle <- import_coding_book("REC_reconstruction_master_coding_book.xlsx",
                             project = project)
validation_summary(bundle$validation)

diagnostics <- run_lbsr_diagnostics(
  bundle,
  respondent_n = 66,
  claimed_item_counts = c(28, 62)
)
print(diagnostics)

project <- record_decision(
  project,
  issue_id = "CON-COUNT-001",
  actor = "Methodological owner",
  alternatives = "28 original items | 62 original items | 28-item reduced model",
  decision = "Retain 62 as the source inventory; investigate 28 as a reduced model",
  rationale = "The master coding book and thesis result tables contain 62 indicators."
)
```

Warnings about stacked observations are expected in the canonical case because
reported values such as 198 and 396 represent pooled item responses rather than
respondent counts. Such warnings document the legacy evidence; they do not
convert 198 into a respondent sample size.
